_readme: The Languages of Ayuma, transcribed from DJ's family-tree chart (1765-AFA snapshot, matching config.presentYear). Modelled on a classic Indo-European tree: roots + subfamily boxes sit in antiquity, the 60 numbered tip languages are all recent, and a representative selection of flagships carry named Ancient/Classical -> Old -> Middle -> Modern stage chains (straight-down columns with clean hand-overs). NOTE: all born/died years are INVENTED placeholders (staggered, not real Andah chronology) added at DJ's request from a dateless cladogram — replace with real dates when known. Disambiguations from the chart: first "Southern Yvian" -> "Northern Yvian"; the two "Hadan" tips are ids hadan-yvian / hadan-fermere.
